refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under controlled duty cycle conditions with continuous telemetry capture of cycle start/end timestamps, segmented by phase (load, travel, dump). Statistical analysis of p95 percentile against CT_target baseline. Comparison of autonomous vs. tele-operated vs. manual modes if applicable.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump motions with deterministic cycle phase timing.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall maintain operator command responsiveness with latency ≤ latency_budget to avoid cycle time degradation.
FR-2.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time penalty.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration) to enable KPI tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-2.A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and motion profiles; implement tele-operation with low-latency network link; enforce safety interlocks with minimal timing overhead.
DP-2.B: Implement cycle time prediction and adaptive duty cycle scheduling to meet p95 target through software-based cycle optimization and operator coaching.

DP-2.C: Decompose cycle time into vehicle motion DP, teleoperation responsiveness DP, and safety constraint DP as independent design parameters with explicit timing budgets and integration verification.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2.C: Decompose cycle time into three coupled design parameters: (1) Vehicle motion subsystem shall deliver load/travel/dump phases with specified phase durations and motion profiles; (2) Teleoperation subsystem shall maintain command-to-actuation latency ≤ latency_budget; (3) Safety services subsystem shall enforce safety constraints with overhead ≤ safety_overhead_budget. Cycle time KPI is achieved through integration of these three DPs with explicit timing budgets that sum to ≤ CT_target.

potential_sources: Vehicle motion DP and Teleoperation DP are coupled: operator latency directly impacts cycle phase duration; tele-operator skill/responsiveness affects travel and dump timing.
Safety services DP and Vehicle motion DP are coupled: safety interlocks (e.g., load stability checks, proximity warnings) may delay phase transitions, increasing cycle time.

Teleoperation DP and Safety services DP are coupled: safety alerts transmitted via network link may compete with command latency budget; safety-driven motion constraints (speed limits, dwell times) affect operator control authority.
coupling_check.notes: The three DPs are not independent. Cycle time is an emergent property of their interaction. Coupling is acceptable if explicitly managed via timing budgets and integration testing, but requires careful allocation and verification. Recommend proceeding with ZIGZAG_DOWN to decompose each DP further and establish coupling constraints.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle hardware capabilities (hydraulic response time, motor acceleration limits) and network latency constraints.
Assumption: Operator skill and tele-operation latency are controllable variables; training and network infrastructure can be optimized.
Risk: Safety constraints may impose timing overhead that exceeds safety_overhead_budget, forcing trade-off between safety and cycle time KPI.
Risk: Network latency variability (jitter) may cause p95 cycle time to exceed target even if mean latency is acceptable.
Risk: Duty cycle definition may not match actual site conditions, invalidating field verification.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Establish explicit timing budgets for each DP before decomposition: (1) Vehicle motion phase duration budget; (2) Teleoperation latency budget (command-to-actuation); (3) Safety overhead budget. Quantify coupling constraints (e.g., 'safety alert latency shall not exceed 100 ms to avoid operator confusion'). Revise DP-2.C to include these budgets as design constraints.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution IM is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the cycle time KPI measurement, reporting, and integration verification. The NGHE Solution IM is responsible for ensuring that the three subsystem DPs are correctly composed to achieve the p95 ≤ CT_target acceptance criterion.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration, p95 statistics) to Site Manager and System Engineer for KPI tracking and performance analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle tasking (load location, dump location, cycle parameters) from Site Manager or autonomous planning subsystem.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs safety alerts (e.g., load instability, proximity warnings) to Safety Manager and Operator via Safety Services subsystem.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for tele-operation command/feedback and telemetry transmission between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for cycle time KPI. It owns the integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Minimal diffusion: cycle time measurement and reporting logic is localized to NGHE Solution or delegated to Vehicle telemetry subsystem. High containment: all three subsystem DPs are within NGHE Solution scope.

Vehicle IM owns vehicle motion DP (load/travel/dump phases) but does not own teleoperation latency or safety constraint DPs. Cycle time KPI is partially contained in Vehicle (motion phase timing) but requires integration with Teleoperation and Safety Services. Moderate diffusion: cycle time measurement requires cross-module coordination.

Teleoperation IM owns teleoperation responsiveness DP (command latency) but does not own vehicle motion or safety constraint DPs. Cycle time KPI is partially contained in Teleoperation (latency budget) but depends on Vehicle and Safety Services. High diffusion: cycle time is emergent property of Teleoperation + Vehicle + Safety Services interaction.

Safety Services IM owns safety constraint DP (overhead budget) but does not own vehicle motion or teleoperation DPs. Cycle time KPI is partially contained in Safety Services (safety overhead) but depends on Vehicle and Teleoperation. High diffusion: cycle time is emergent property of Safety Services + Vehicle + Teleoperation interaction.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The cycle time KPI (FR-2) is an emergent property of the NGHE Solution that integrates three subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). No single subsystem owns the KPI; instead, NGHE Solution owns the integration responsibility and must establish explicit timing budgets and coupling constraints for each subsystem DP. The NGHE Solution IM is responsible for cycle time measurement, reporting, and verification that p95 ≤ CT_target is achieved through correct composition of subsystem DPs.
containment_risks: Risk: Cycle time KPI is diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. If timing budgets are not explicitly allocated and enforced, cycle time may exceed target due to uncontrolled coupling.

Risk: NGHE Solution IM may lack sufficient authority or instrumentation to measure and enforce cycle time KPI if Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services operate independently without integration verification.
Risk: Telemetry interface may not provide sufficient granularity (phase-level timestamps) to diagnose cycle time failures and attribute them to specific subsystem DPs.
Risk: Network Link latency variability may cause p95 cycle time to exceed target even if mean latency is acceptable; NGHE Solution must account for latency jitter in timing budgets.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems before decomposition. Define NGHE Solution integration verification plan that includes: (1) Phase-level telemetry capture and p95 analysis; (2) Coupling constraint verification (e.g., safety alert latency impact on operator response time); (3) Duty cycle validation against site conditions. Assign cycle time KPI ownership to NGHE Solution with clear accountability for integration and verification.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is refined with measurable acceptance criterion (p95 ≤ CT_target) and explicit duty cycle context. DP-2.C decomposes cycle time into three coupled DPs (vehicle motion, teleoperation responsiveness, safety constraint) with explicit timing budgets. However, the coupling between DPs is not fully resolved: vehicle motion DP and teleoperation DP are coupled (operator latency affects cycle phase duration); safety services DP and vehicle motion DP are coupled (safety interlocks may delay phase transitions). The FR-DP mapping is logically sound but requires explicit coupling constraints and timing budget allocation to be verifiable. Recommend ZIGZAG_DOWN to establish coupling constraints and timing budgets before proceeding to IM assignment.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time KPI is diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services IMs. No single IM owns the KPI; instead, NGHE Solution owns integration responsibility. This diffusion is acceptable if explicit timing budgets and coupling constraints are established and enforced.

Telemetry interface must provide phase-level timestamps and p95 statistics to enable cycle time KPI verification. If Telemetry interface is limited to aggregate cycle time only, root cause analysis of cycle time failures will be difficult.

Network Link latency variability may cause p95 cycle time to exceed target. NGHE Solution must account for latency jitter in timing budgets and verify that p95 cycle time is achievable under realistic network conditions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM is the appropriate top-level container for cycle time KPI. It owns integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems and is responsible for establishing timing budgets, coupling constraints, and integration verification. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services IMs own their respective DPs but must operate within NGHE Solution timing budgets. Recommend explicit allocation of cycle time budget to each subsystem IM with clear accountability for meeting allocated budget.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI verification requires phase-level telemetry (load start/end, travel start/end, dump start/end timestamps) and statistical analysis of p95 percentile. Feasibility depends on: (1) Vehicle telemetry subsystem capability to capture phase timestamps with sufficient precision; (2) Telemetry interface capability to transmit phase-level data; (3) NGHE Solution capability to aggregate and analyze telemetry data. Current interface definitions (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) do not explicitly specify phase-level telemetry granularity. Recommend clarifying Telemetry interface specification to include phase-level timestamps.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI allocation to subsystem IMs is not clear. Recommend explicit allocation: (1) Vehicle IM shall achieve load/travel/dump phase durations within specified budgets; (2) Teleoperation IM shall maintain command-to-actuation latency ≤ latency_budget; (3) Safety Services IM shall enforce safety constraints with overhead ≤ safety_overhead_budget. NGHE Solution IM shall verify that sum of subsystem budgets ≤ CT_target and that p95 cycle time is achieved under duty cycle conditions.

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit timing budgets for each subsystem DP before decomposition. Quantify: (1) Vehicle motion phase duration budget (load, travel, dump); (2) Teleoperation latency budget (command-to-actuation); (3) Safety overhead budget. Ensure sum of budgets ≤ CT_target with margin for variability.

Define coupling constraints between DPs: (1) Teleoperation latency impact on operator response time and cycle phase duration; (2) Safety alert latency impact on operator awareness and decision-making; (3) Safety interlock impact on phase transition timing.

Clarify Telemetry interface specification to include phase-level timestamps (load start/end, travel start/end, dump start/end) and p95 statistics calculation. Ensure Vehicle telemetry subsystem can capture and report phase-level data.

Establish NGHE Solution integration verification plan that includes: (1) Phase-level telemetry capture and p95 analysis; (2) Coupling constraint verification; (3) Duty cycle validation against site conditions; (4) Network latency jitter analysis and impact on p95 cycle time.

Assign explicit cycle time KPI ownership to NGHE Solution with clear accountability for integration and verification. Define escalation path if subsystem budgets are exceeded or coupling constraints are violated.

decision.justification: FR-2 (cycle time KPI) is a coupled, emergent property that depends on correct integration of three subsystem DPs (vehicle motion, teleoperation responsiveness, safety constraint). The FR-DP mapping is logically sound but requires explicit coupling constraints and timing budget allocation to be verifiable. The DP-IM assignment is appropriate (NGHE Solution as top-level container) but depends on establishing clear timing budgets and integration verification criteria. Recommend ZIGZAG_DOWN to decompose each subsystem DP (FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3) and establish explicit timing budgets and coupling constraints before proceeding to detailed design. This will enable clear allocation of cycle time KPI responsibility to subsystem IMs and establish verifiable acceptance criteria for each subsystem.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump motions with deterministic cycle phase timing (load_duration ≤ load_budget, travel_duration ≤ travel_budget, dump_duration ≤ dump_budget).

FR-2.2: The teleoperation subsystem shall maintain operator command responsiveness with latency ≤ latency_budget (command-to-actuation latency measured end-to-end including network link and vehicle actuation response).

FR-2.3: The safety services subsystem shall enforce safety constraints (load stability, proximity warnings, speed limits) without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time penalty (measured as additional delay imposed by safety interlocks and alerts).

FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report cycle time telemetry (phase timestamps, total cycle duration, p95 percentile) via Telemetry interface to enable KPI tracking and root cause analysis of cycle time failures.

revise.why: DP-2.C requires explicit timing budgets and coupling constraints to be verifiable. Recommend revising DP-2.C to include: (1) Explicit allocation of cycle time budget to vehicle motion, teleoperation latency, and safety overhead; (2) Quantified coupling constraints (e.g., 'teleoperation latency shall not exceed 200 ms to avoid operator confusion'; 'safety alert latency shall not exceed 100 ms'); (3) Integration verification criteria (e.g., 'p95 cycle time shall be measured under duty cycle conditions and shall not exceed CT_target'). This will enable clear decomposition of FR-2 into FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4 with explicit acceptance criteria for each sub-FR.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle hardware capabilities (hydraulic response time, motor acceleration limits) and network latency constraints.
Operator skill and tele-operation latency are controllable variables; training and network infrastructure can be optimized.
Duty cycle definition matches actual site conditions; field verification will validate this assumption.
Safety constraints can be enforced with overhead ≤ safety_overhead_budget; trade-off between safety and cycle time is acceptable within this budget.
Network latency variability (jitter) is bounded and can be accounted for in timing budgets; p95 cycle time is achievable under realistic network conditions.
Telemetry interface can provide phase-level timestamps with sufficient precision (e.g., ±100 ms) to enable p95 analysis and root cause diagnosis.
open_risks: Risk: Safety constraints may impose timing overhead that exceeds safety_overhead_budget, forcing trade-off between safety and cycle time KPI. Mitigation: Establish safety_overhead_budget early in design and validate through simulation and field trials.

Risk: Network latency variability may cause p95 cycle time to exceed target even if mean latency is acceptable. Mitigation: Analyze network latency distribution (not just mean) and account for jitter in timing budgets.

Risk: Operator skill and tele-operation latency may vary significantly across operators and site conditions. Mitigation: Establish operator training program and validate cycle time KPI across multiple operators and duty cycles.

Risk: Duty cycle definition may not match actual site conditions, invalidating field verification. Mitigation: Validate duty cycle definition with Site Manager and conduct field trials under representative conditions.

Risk: Coupling between vehicle motion, teleoperation, and safety constraint DPs may be underestimated, leading to cycle time failures. Mitigation: Establish explicit coupling constraints and verify through integration testing.
evidence_needed: Quantified timing budgets for vehicle motion (load, travel, dump phase durations), teleoperation latency, and safety overhead. These budgets must sum to ≤ CT_target with margin for variability.
Network latency analysis: mean, standard deviation, p95, p99 percentiles under representative site conditions. Verify that p95 teleoperation latency is within latency_budget.

Safety constraint analysis: identify all safety interlocks and alerts that may impact cycle time; quantify timing overhead for each constraint; verify that total safety overhead ≤ safety_overhead_budget.
Telemetry interface specification: define phase-level timestamps (load start/end, travel start/end, dump start/end), precision requirements, and p95 statistics calculation method.
Integration verification plan: define test scenarios, acceptance criteria, and measurement methodology for verifying that p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under duty cycle conditions.

Field trial results: measure cycle time KPI under representative site conditions with multiple operators and duty cycles. Validate that p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target and identify root causes of any failures.
